title: Atylus roseus
taxonomy: Atylus roseus is a species of isopod in the family Janiridae. It belongs to the phylum Arthropoda, class Malacostraca, and order Isopoda. The phylum Arthropoda is a large group of animals that also includes insects, arachnids, and crustaceans. Atylus roseus is further classified within the family Janiridae, which is a group of small, deep-sea isopods. The classification of Atylus roseus provides valuable information about its evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Atylus roseus, also known as Red Sea rose or Egyptian rose, is a flowering plant species native to the Middle East and North Africa. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate various ailments including digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Atylus roseus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Atylus roseus is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often marketed for their potential to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
